# Story 9.4: App Store Readiness - Critical Gaps (IMPLEMENTATION GUIDE)

**Epic:** Epic 9 - Production Launch & App Store Publishing
**Story ID:** 9.4
**Priority:** M (Must Have) - **BLOCKS TESTFLIGHT & SUBMISSION**
**Estimate:** 13 story points
**Status:** ready-for-dev
**Created:** 2025-12-23
**Generated By:** Bob (SM Agent) via BMAD Ultimate Context Engine + YOLO Mode

---

## 📋 User Story

**As a** product manager
**I want to** complete all critical App Store requirements before TestFlight submission
**So that** we can successfully publish Weave to the App Store without rejection or delays

---

## 🎯 Story Overview

**Context:** Story 9.1 (Production Backend Deployment) is complete. Story 9.3 (App Store Compliance) handles legal documents and metadata. **Story 9.4 fills the critical gaps** that block TestFlight Beta Testing (Story 9.6) and App Store Submission (Story 9.8).

**Scope:** This story implements 10 CRITICAL gaps identified in the original 9.4 spec that are **BLOCKERS for App Store submission**:

1. ✅ **In-App Purchases (IAP)** - RevenueCat integration for subscription management
2. ✅ **Privacy Manifest** - Apple's required `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` file
3. ✅ **App Icons** - Production-quality icon set for iOS
4. ✅ **App Store Screenshots** - 6 required screenshots (6.7" + 5.5")
5. ✅ **Push Notifications Setup** - APNs certificate and token management
6. ✅ **Deep Linking** - Universal Links for `weavelight://` URLs
7. ✅ **Settings Screen** - Account management (delete account, export data, privacy links)
8. ✅ **Error Tracking** - Sentry integration for production monitoring
9. ✅ **Analytics** - PostHog integration for user insights (optional)
10. ✅ **Production Build Configuration** - EAS build profiles for App Store submission

**Non-Scope (handled by other stories):**
- ❌ Legal documents (Privacy Policy, ToS) → Story 9.3
- ❌ Backend deployment → Story 9.1 (DONE)
- ❌ Database production setup → Story 9.2
- ❌ TestFlight beta testing → Story 9.6
- ❌ Final App Store submission → Story 9.8

---

## ✅ Acceptance Criteria

### 1. In-App Purchases (IAP) - RevenueCat Integration

**Context:** Apple requires all digital content subscriptions to use Apple IAP (not Stripe/PayPal). RevenueCat simplifies IAP implementation and cross-platform subscription management.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] RevenueCat account created (free tier: up to $10K monthly revenue)
- [ ] iOS app configured in RevenueCat dashboard
- [ ] 3 subscription products created in App Store Connect:
  - **Free Trial:** 10-day free trial (no charge upfront)
  - **Pro Monthly:** $9.99/month after trial
  - **Pro Annual:** $99.99/year after trial (2 months free)
- [ ] Product IDs registered in App Store Connect:
  - `com.weavelight.app.pro.monthly`
  - `com.weavelight.app.pro.annual`
  - `com.weavelight.app.trial.10day`
- [ ] RevenueCat SDK integrated in React Native app
- [ ] Subscription UI implemented in Settings screen:
  - Display current plan (Free Trial, Pro, Expired)
  - "Upgrade to Pro" button (opens subscription modal)
  - "Manage Subscription" link (opens App Store subscriptions)
  - "Restore Purchases" button (for re-installing users)
- [ ] Backend API updated to check subscription status:
  - `GET /api/user/subscription` - Returns tier (free/pro), expiry date
  - Middleware checks RevenueCat API for active subscription
- [ ] Rate limiting updated to respect subscription tier:
  - Free: 10 AI calls/day (premium questions), 40/day (free questions)
  - Pro: 5000 AI calls/month
- [ ] Subscription verification tested:
  - Sandbox environment (test users in App Store Connect)
  - Production environment (real purchases after release)

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/src/services/subscription.ts` - RevenueCat API client
- `weave-mobile/src/hooks/useSubscription.ts` - TanStack Query hook
- `weave-mobile/src/screens/SubscriptionModal.tsx` - Subscription paywall UI
- `weave-mobile/app/(tabs)/settings/subscription.tsx` - Settings subscription screen
- `weave-api/app/api/subscription_router.py` - Backend subscription verification
- `weave-api/app/middleware/subscription_middleware.py` - Tier-based rate limiting

**Testing:**
- [ ] Test subscription purchase in Sandbox mode
- [ ] Test subscription restoration after reinstall
- [ ] Test tier-based rate limiting (free vs pro)
- [ ] Test subscription expiry handling

---

### 2. Privacy Manifest - PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy

**Context:** Apple requires all apps to declare data collection practices in `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` (iOS 17+). Missing this file will result in App Store rejection.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Create `weave-mobile/ios/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` file
- [ ] Declare all required privacy practices:
  - **Data Collected:**
    - Contact Info: Email address (authentication)
    - User Content: Goals, journal entries, images, audio (core features)
    - Identifiers: Device ID, User ID (analytics, Sentry)
    - Usage Data: App interactions (PostHog analytics)
  - **Data Usage:**
    - App Functionality: All user content used for AI features
    - Analytics: PostHog tracks usage patterns
    - Product Personalization: AI coach learns from user data
  - **Third-Party SDKs:**
    - Sentry: Error tracking (device info, crash logs)
    - PostHog: Analytics (usage events, device info)
    - RevenueCat: Subscription management (purchase history)
    - Expo: App updates, push notifications
- [ ] Verify compliance with App Store Connect's privacy questionnaire
- [ ] Link privacy manifest to Xcode project:
  - Open `weave-mobile/ios/weave.xcodeproj` in Xcode
  - Add `PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` to project resources
  - Verify file appears in "Copy Bundle Resources" build phase

**Implementation File:**
- `weave-mobile/ios/PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy` (NEW - XML plist format)

**Testing:**
- [ ] Build iOS app and verify privacy manifest is included in bundle
- [ ] Validate privacy manifest with App Store Connect submission

**Reference:**
- Apple Privacy Manifest Guide: https://developer.apple.com/documentation/bundleresources/privacy_manifest_files

---

### 3. App Icons - Production-Quality Icon Set

**Context:** Weave currently uses placeholder icons. Apple requires production-quality icons in multiple sizes for App Store submission.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Design production app icon (1024x1024 px):
  - Logo concept: Interlocking threads forming a "W" or abstract weave pattern
  - Color scheme: Primary blue (#3B72F6) with gradient or solid background
  - No transparency (iOS requirement)
  - No text in icon (iOS guideline)
- [ ] Generate icon set using Expo:
  ```bash
  # Place 1024x1024 icon at: weave-mobile/assets/icon.png
  npx expo prebuild --clean
  # Auto-generates all iOS icon sizes
  ```
- [ ] Verify icon appears correctly:
  - Home screen (60x60, 120x120, 180x180)
  - Settings (29x29, 58x58, 87x87)
  - Spotlight (40x40, 80x80, 120x120)
  - App Store (1024x1024)
- [ ] Update `app.json` with final icon path:
  ```json
  {
    "expo": {
      "icon": "./assets/icon.png",
      "ios": {
        "icon": "./assets/icon.png"
      }
    }
  }
  ```

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/assets/icon.png` (1024x1024 px, production-quality)
- `weave-mobile/app.json` (updated icon path)

**Design Tools:**
- Figma template: https://www.figma.com/community/file/1234567890/ios-app-icon-template
- Icon generator: https://appicon.co/ (generates all sizes from 1024x1024)

**Testing:**
- [ ] Build iOS app and verify icon appears on device home screen
- [ ] Test icon in all contexts (home screen, settings, spotlight)

---

### 4. App Store Screenshots - 6 Required Screenshots

**Context:** Apple requires 6 screenshots (6.7" iPhone Pro Max) and 6 screenshots (5.5" iPhone 8 Plus) for App Store listing. Screenshots must show real app UI (no mockups).

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Design 6 key screenshots showcasing core features:
  1. **Welcome Screen** - Value prop: "Turn vague goals into daily wins in 10 days"
  2. **Goal Creation** - Show AI-powered goal breakdown (needle → binds)
  3. **Daily Thread** - Today's binds with completion status
  4. **Journal Entry** - Daily reflection with fulfillment score
  5. **Progress Dashboard** - Heat map, consistency stats, weave character evolution
  6. **AI Coach** - Dream Self chat with personalized coaching
- [ ] Capture screenshots on 6.7" iPhone (iPhone 15 Pro Max simulator)
- [ ] Capture screenshots on 5.5" iPhone (iPhone 8 Plus simulator)
- [ ] Add overlay text to screenshots (optional but recommended):
  - Screenshot 1: "10-Day Identity Transformation"
  - Screenshot 2: "AI Breaks Goals into Daily Habits"
  - Screenshot 3: "Complete Binds Every Day"
  - Screenshot 4: "Reflect & Track Fulfillment"
  - Screenshot 5: "Visualize Your Progress"
  - Screenshot 6: "Chat with Your Dream Self"
- [ ] Upload screenshots to App Store Connect

**Implementation:**
- Use React Native's Screenshot API or Simulator's "Save Screenshot" feature
- Edit screenshots with Figma/Photoshop to add overlay text

**Testing:**
- [ ] Preview screenshots in App Store Connect
- [ ] Verify screenshots match actual app UI (Apple rejects fake mockups)

**Reference:**
- App Store Screenshot Guidelines: https://developer.apple.com/app-store/product-page/

---

### 5. Push Notifications Setup - APNs Certificate

**Context:** Expo Push Notifications require Apple Push Notification service (APNs) certificate. Without this, push notifications will fail in production.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Generate APNs Key in Apple Developer Portal:
  - Go to https://developer.apple.com/account/resources/authkeys/list
  - Click "Create a Key"
  - Name: "Weave Push Notifications"
  - Enable: "Apple Push Notifications service (APNs)"
  - Download `.p8` key file (save securely - can't re-download)
- [ ] Configure Expo Push in `app.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "expo": {
      "ios": {
        "infoPlist": {
          "UIBackgroundModes": ["remote-notification"]
        }
      },
      "plugins": [
        [
          "expo-notifications",
          {
            "icon": "./assets/notification-icon.png",
            "sounds": ["./assets/notification-sound.wav"]
          }
        ]
      ]
    }
  }
  ```
- [ ] Upload APNs Key to Expo:
  ```bash
  eas credentials
  # Select: iOS → Push Notifications → Upload APNs Key
  # Provide: Key ID, Team ID, .p8 file
  ```
- [ ] Test push notifications in production:
  - Send test notification via Expo Push API
  - Verify notification appears on device

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/app.json` (updated with push notification config)
- APNs Key stored securely in Expo credentials

**Testing:**
- [ ] Send test push notification to physical iOS device
- [ ] Verify notification appears when app is backgrounded
- [ ] Verify notification deep links to correct screen

**Reference:**
- Expo Push Notifications: https://docs.expo.dev/push-notifications/overview/

---

### 6. Deep Linking - Universal Links

**Context:** Weave uses `weavelight://` deep links for navigation (e.g., `weavelight://goals/123`). Universal Links require Apple's App Site Association (AASA) file.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Create AASA file at `weavelight.app/.well-known/apple-app-site-association`:
  ```json
  {
    "applinks": {
      "apps": [],
      "details": [
        {
          "appID": "TEAM_ID.com.weavelight.app",
          "paths": ["/goals/*", "/journal/*", "/settings/*"]
        }
      ]
    }
  }
  ```
- [ ] Host AASA file on production domain (weavelight.app)
- [ ] Configure Associated Domains in `app.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "expo": {
      "ios": {
        "associatedDomains": ["applinks:weavelight.app"]
      }
    }
  }
  ```
- [ ] Test universal links:
  - Tap link in Safari: `https://weavelight.app/goals/123`
  - Should open Weave app (not Safari web view)

**Implementation Files:**
- `weavelight.app/.well-known/apple-app-site-association` (NEW - hosted on production site)
- `weave-mobile/app.json` (updated with associated domains)

**Testing:**
- [ ] Test universal link from Safari on iOS device
- [ ] Verify app opens to correct screen (e.g., goal detail)

**Reference:**
- Universal Links Guide: https://developer.apple.com/ios/universal-links/

---

### 7. Settings Screen - Account Management

**Context:** Apple requires apps to provide:
1. Account deletion
2. Data export (GDPR compliance)
3. Privacy policy link
4. Terms of service link

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Create Settings screen at `weave-mobile/app/(tabs)/settings/index.tsx`
- [ ] Implement account management features:
  - **Account Section:**
    - Display user email
    - "Edit Profile" button → Edit name, email
    - "Delete Account" button → Confirmation dialog → API call → Logout
  - **Subscription Section:**
    - Display current plan (Free/Pro)
    - "Upgrade to Pro" button (if free)
    - "Manage Subscription" link → Opens App Store subscriptions
  - **Privacy Section:**
    - "Privacy Policy" link → Opens web view or Safari
    - "Terms of Service" link → Opens web view or Safari
    - "Export My Data" button → Generates JSON export → Shares via native share sheet
  - **Support Section:**
    - "Contact Support" button → Opens email client (support@weavelight.app)
    - "App Version" (display current version from app.json)
- [ ] Backend APIs:
  - `DELETE /api/user/account` - Delete user account (soft delete)
  - `GET /api/user/export` - Export user data as JSON
- [ ] Account deletion flow:
  - Confirmation dialog: "Are you sure? This cannot be undone."
  - API call: `DELETE /api/user/account`
  - Response: `{"status": "deleted", "message": "Account deleted successfully"}`
  - Logout user and redirect to welcome screen
- [ ] Data export flow:
  - API call: `GET /api/user/export`
  - Response: JSON with all user data (profile, goals, completions, journal entries)
  - Native share sheet to export JSON file

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/app/(tabs)/settings/index.tsx` - Settings home screen
- `weave-mobile/app/(tabs)/settings/account.tsx` - Account management screen
- `weave-mobile/app/(tabs)/settings/privacy.tsx` - Privacy settings screen
- `weave-api/app/api/user_router.py` - Account deletion and data export APIs

**Testing:**
- [ ] Test account deletion (soft delete in database)
- [ ] Test data export (verify JSON contains all user data)
- [ ] Test privacy policy link opens correctly
- [ ] Test subscription management link opens App Store

---

### 8. Error Tracking - Sentry Integration

**Context:** Sentry is already configured in `app.json` but needs production setup (DSN, environment, error boundaries).

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Create Sentry project for iOS production:
  - Go to https://sentry.io/organizations/jackluo/projects/
  - Create new project: "weavelight-ios-production"
  - Copy DSN: `https://xxx@sentry.io/xxx`
- [ ] Configure Sentry in `app.json`:
  ```json
  {
    "expo": {
      "plugins": [
        [
          "@sentry/react-native/expo",
          {
            "url": "https://sentry.io/",
            "project": "weavelight-ios-production",
            "organization": "jackluo"
          }
        ]
      ]
    }
  }
  ```
- [ ] Add Sentry environment variables to `.env`:
  ```bash
  EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN=https://xxx@sentry.io/xxx
  EXPO_PUBLIC_ENV=production
  ```
- [ ] Wrap root app in Sentry error boundary:
  ```typescript
  // weave-mobile/app/_layout.tsx
  import * as Sentry from '@sentry/react-native';

  Sentry.init({
    dsn: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_SENTRY_DSN,
    environment: process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_ENV,
    enableAutoSessionTracking: true,
    tracesSampleRate: 0.2,
  });

  export default function RootLayout() {
    return (
      <Sentry.ErrorBoundary fallback={<ErrorScreen />}>
        {/* App content */}
      </Sentry.ErrorBoundary>
    );
  }
  ```
- [ ] Test error tracking:
  - Trigger test error in app
  - Verify error appears in Sentry dashboard

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/app.json` (updated Sentry config)
- `weave-mobile/.env` (added Sentry DSN)
- `weave-mobile/app/_layout.tsx` (wrapped in Sentry error boundary)

**Testing:**
- [ ] Trigger test error (e.g., undefined variable access)
- [ ] Verify error appears in Sentry dashboard with stack trace

**Reference:**
- Sentry React Native Docs: https://docs.sentry.io/platforms/react-native/

---

### 9. Analytics - PostHog Integration (Optional)

**Context:** PostHog provides user analytics (event tracking, funnels, retention). Free tier: 1M events/month.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Create PostHog project:
  - Go to https://app.posthog.com/
  - Create project: "Weave iOS Production"
  - Copy API key: `phc_xxx`
- [ ] Install PostHog SDK:
  ```bash
  cd weave-mobile
  npm install posthog-react-native
  ```
- [ ] Initialize PostHog in app:
  ```typescript
  // weave-mobile/app/_layout.tsx
  import PostHog from 'posthog-react-native';

  const posthog = new PostHog(
    process.env.EXPO_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_API_KEY,
    {
      host: 'https://app.posthog.com',
      autocapture: true,
    }
  );

  export default function RootLayout() {
    useEffect(() => {
      posthog.capture('app_opened');
    }, []);

    return <App />;
  }
  ```
- [ ] Track key events:
  - `goal_created` - User creates a new goal
  - `bind_completed` - User completes a bind
  - `journal_submitted` - User submits daily journal
  - `subscription_purchased` - User upgrades to Pro
- [ ] Add PostHog environment variable to `.env`:
  ```bash
  EXPO_PUBLIC_POSTHOG_API_KEY=phc_xxx
  ```

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/app/_layout.tsx` (PostHog initialization)
- `weave-mobile/src/services/analytics.ts` - Analytics helper functions
- `weave-mobile/.env` (added PostHog API key)

**Testing:**
- [ ] Track test event and verify in PostHog dashboard
- [ ] Verify autocapture tracks screen views

**Reference:**
- PostHog React Native Docs: https://posthog.com/docs/libraries/react-native

---

### 10. Production Build Configuration - EAS Build

**Context:** Expo Application Services (EAS) builds production-ready iOS/Android apps. Current `eas.json` is minimal and needs production configuration.

**Requirements:**
- [ ] Update `eas.json` with production build profiles:
  ```json
  {
    "cli": {
      "version": ">= 16.28.0",
      "appVersionSource": "remote"
    },
    "build": {
      "development": {
        "developmentClient": true,
        "distribution": "internal",
        "ios": {
          "simulator": true
        }
      },
      "preview": {
        "distribution": "internal",
        "ios": {
          "simulator": false
        }
      },
      "production": {
        "autoIncrement": true,
        "ios": {
          "simulator": false,
          "bundleIdentifier": "com.weavelight.app"
        },
        "env": {
          "EXPO_PUBLIC_ENV": "production"
        }
      }
    },
    "submit": {
      "production": {
        "ios": {
          "appleId": "jack@weavelight.app",
          "ascAppId": "PENDING",
          "appleTeamId": "PENDING"
        }
      }
    }
  }
  ```
- [ ] Create production build:
  ```bash
  eas build --platform ios --profile production
  ```
- [ ] Wait for build to complete (15-30 minutes)
- [ ] Download IPA file from EAS dashboard
- [ ] Test IPA on physical device (TestFlight or Ad Hoc distribution)

**Implementation Files:**
- `weave-mobile/eas.json` (updated with production profiles)

**Testing:**
- [ ] Build production IPA and install on physical device
- [ ] Verify all features work (subscriptions, push notifications, deep links)

**Reference:**
- EAS Build Docs: https://docs.expo.dev/build/introduction/

---

## 🔍 Developer Context & Guardrails

### Architecture Compliance

**Story 1.5.2: Backend Patterns**
- All new API endpoints MUST use `Depends(get_current_user)` for authentication
- Use `snake_case` for all API parameters
- Use Pydantic models: `{Resource}Create`, `{Resource}Response`
- Error handling: Standard codes (VALIDATION_ERROR, NOT_FOUND, RATE_LIMIT_EXCEEDED)

**Story 0.4: Row Level Security (RLS)**
- All user data tables have RLS policies enabled
- Account deletion = soft delete (`deleted_at` timestamp, not hard DELETE)
- Data export queries MUST respect RLS (user can only export own data)

**Story 6.1: AI Rate Limiting**
- Free tier: 10 AI calls/day (premium), 40/day (free)
- Pro tier: 5000 AI calls/month
- Rate limiting enforced in `TieredRateLimiter` class

### Critical Dependencies

**Story 9.3: App Store Compliance (MUST BE COMPLETE FIRST)**
- Privacy Policy published (weavelight.app/privacy)
- Terms of Service published (weavelight.app/terms)
- App Store Connect metadata filled in

**Story 9.1: Production Backend Deployment (COMPLETE)**
- Backend deployed to Railway: `https://weave-api-production.railway.app`
- Health check endpoint: `/health`

### Testing Strategy

**Unit Tests:**
- RevenueCat: Mock API responses in `__tests__/services/subscription.test.ts`
- Data export: Verify JSON structure matches schema
- Account deletion: Verify soft delete (not hard delete)

**Integration Tests:**
- IAP: Test subscription purchase in Sandbox mode
- Push notifications: Send test notification via Expo Push API
- Deep linking: Test universal links from Safari

**Manual Tests (Required):**
- Install production build on physical device
- Test subscription purchase with real Apple ID (Sandbox)
- Test push notifications
- Test deep linking
- Test account deletion and data export
